title: Withdraw ₳314,800 for PyCardano administered by Intersect

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (enacted), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds PyCardano which will provide the following services: ​ PyCardano is a lightweight Python library that enables developers to interact seamlessly with the Cardano blockchain." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw ₳314,800 for PyCardano administered by Intersect

Deliverables: This treasury withdrawal funds PyCardano which will provide the following services: ​ PyCardano is a lightweight Python library that enables developers to interact seamlessly with the Cardano blockchain., It facilitates the creation and signing of transactions without reliance on external serialization tools, thereby simplifying the development process and broadening accessibility for Python developers., Given the dynamic nature of blockchain technology, particularly with Cardano's ongoing protocol upgrades and hard forks, it is imperative to maintain and update PyCardano to ensure continuous compatibility and functionality.​ This proposal aims to: Ensure C..., This Treasury Withdrawal is submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal funds PyCardano which will provide the following services: ​ PyCardano is a lightweight Python library that enables developers to interact seamlessly with the Cardano blockchain.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): It facilitates the creation and signing of transactions without reliance on external serialization tools, thereby simplifying the development process and broadening accessibility for Python developers.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Given the dynamic nature of blockchain technology, particularly with Cardano's ongoing protocol upgrades and hard forks, it is imperative to maintain and update PyCardano to ensure continuous compatibility and functionality.​ This proposal aims to: Ensure C...
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This Treasury Withdrawal is submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): This proposal aims to solve the following problem: The Cardano ecosystem primarily offers development tools and libraries in Haskell and JavaScript, creating a significant barrier for Python developers who wish to engage with the platform.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): This limitation hinders the growth and diversification of the developer community within the Cardano ecosystem.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds PyCardano which will provide the following services: ​ PyCardano is a lightweight Python library that enables developers to interact seamlessly with the Cardano blockchain." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (missing complete proposal summary) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
Gate disbursement on milestones that must be met before money moves.
Provide a clawback or refund path if the work is not delivered.
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
delivery controls, milestones, assurance, or remedy path

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, whose voting CLOSED with a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.

pending_candidate.note: Voting on this action is still open; its interim tally does not set the operative ceiling. It becomes the pin only if it closes with Yes > No.
